title: The Oldest Cat in the World and the Secrets of Feline Longevity
description: What is the oldest cat that has ever lived? Creme Puff lived 38 years. Discover the secrets of feline longevity, how to keep your cat living for years to come, and what research reveals about aging.

segments.voice: The oldest domestic cat recorded in history was Creme Puff, who lived in Texas for 38 years and 3 days. She was born on August 3, 1967, and passed away on August 6, 2005.
Creme Puff was fed an unconventional diet: it included bacon, eggs, broccoli, and aspirin for cats. Her owner, Jake Perry, meticulously took care of her diet and health for decades.
Although 38 years is extraordinary, the average age of a domestic cat is 12 to 18 years. Some cats can reach 20 or even 25 years with proper care.
To convert a cat's age to equivalent human age, it's not just a matter of multiplying by 7. Cats age faster initially. A 1-year-old cat is equivalent to a 15-year-old human.
A 2-year-old cat is equivalent to a 24-year-old human. After that, each cat year is approximately equal to 4-5 human years.
Some of the oldest documented cats include Granpa Rexs Allen, who lived 34 years; Spike, who lived 31 years; and Kitty White, who lived 29 years.
Researchers at the University of Liverpool studied the records of 4,000 cats to identify longevity factors. They found that dental health is an important predictor of longevity.
Cats with healthy teeth lived an average of 3 years longer than those with periodontal disease. Oral health is critical for overall health.
Weight is another important factor. Cats with moderate weight, neither obese nor too thin, lived significantly longer than those who were overweight.
Obese cats have a higher risk of diabetes, heart disease, and joint problems. Maintaining a healthy weight can extend your cat's life by 2-3 years.
Nutrition is fundamental. Cats are obligate carnivores and require high-quality protein. A suitable diet is essential for longevity.
Researchers at the University of California found that cats fed high-protein diets (30%+) lived 2 years longer than those fed low-protein diets.
Exercise is also crucial. Cats that receive environmental enrichment, interactive toys, and regular physical activity lived more years.
Indoor cats live longer than outdoor cats due to protection from hazards and diseases. The average age of an indoor cat is 12-18 years, while outdoor cats average 2-5 years.
Preventive medicine is important. Regular veterinary check-ups, vaccinations, and early treatment of diseases can add years to a cat's life.
Aging cats (over 12 years) are more prone to chronic diseases such as diabetes, kidney disease, and hyperthyroidism. Monitoring is essential.
Mental stimulation also affects longevity. Cats that receive cognitive stimulation, social interaction, and mental enrichment lived more years.

In conclusion, although Creme Puff was exceptional, most cats can live 15-20 years with proper care. Nutrition, exercise, healthy weight, and preventive medicine are the secrets to a long feline life.
